steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The prompt requires emphasizing when Judea Pearl won the award. The notes give exactly one date: 2011. The winning choice must (1) include that date and (2) structure the sentence so the date is prominent.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Judea Pearl is one winner of the A.M. Turing Award.': No date at all, so timing is not emphasized. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It was in 2011 that Judea Pearl won the A.M. Turing Award.': The cleft construction 'It was in 2011 that...' places 2011 in the most emphatic position in the sentence. ✓
  • Choice C — 'The prestigious A.M. Turing Award is given for "major contributions of lasting importance to computing."': No date and no mention of Judea Pearl, so it cannot emphasize timing. ✗
  • Choice D — 'For developing a calculus for probabilistic reasoning, Judea Pearl won the A.M. Turing Award.': This emphasizes why Pearl won, not when — and omits the date entirely. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
